exercises.title: Key Storage Capacity Calculator
exercises.description: Build a tool to calculate storage requirements for PQC key migration.
tasks: Define key sizes for all NIST PQC algorithms
Calculate storage needed for N keys of each type
Factor in backup and redundancy requirements
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict

@dataclass
class KeySizes:
    public_key: int  # bytes
    private_key: int  # bytes
    signature: int  # bytes (for signature algorithms)
    ciphertext: int  # bytes (for KEMs)

ALGORITHM_SIZES: Dict[str, KeySizes] = {
    # Classical
    "ECDSA-P256": KeySizes(64, 32, 64, 0),
    "RSA-2048": KeySizes(256, 1024, 256, 0),
    "X25519": KeySizes(32, 32, 0, 32),
    
    # ML-KEM
    "ML-KEM-512": KeySizes(800, 1632, 0, 768),
    "ML-KEM-768": KeySizes(1184, 2400, 0, 1088),
    "ML-KEM-1024": KeySizes(1568, 3168, 0, 1568),
    
    # ML-DSA
    "ML-DSA-44": KeySizes(1312, 2560, 2420, 0),
    "ML-DSA-65": KeySizes(1952, 4032, 3293, 0),
    "ML-DSA-87": KeySizes(2592, 4896, 4595, 0),
    
    # SLH-DSA (example variants)
    "SLH-DSA-128f": KeySizes(32, 64, 17088, 0),
    "SLH-DSA-256f": KeySizes(64, 128, 49856, 0),
    
    # FN-DSA
    "FN-DSA-512": KeySizes(897, 1281, 666, 0),
    "FN-DSA-1024": KeySizes(1793, 2305, 1280, 0),
}

def calculate_storage(num_keys: int, algorithm: str, 
                      include_backup: bool = True) -> dict:
    """
    Calculate storage requirements for keys.
    
    Args:
        num_keys: Number of key pairs
        algorithm: Algorithm name
        include_backup: Include backup storage (2x)
    
    Returns:
        Storage breakdown in bytes
    """
    sizes = ALGORITHM_SIZES[algorithm]
    
    multiplier = 2 if include_backup else 1
    
    return {
        "algorithm": algorithm,
        "num_keys": num_keys,
        "public_keys_bytes": num_keys * sizes.public_key * multiplier,
        "private_keys_bytes": num_keys * sizes.private_key * multiplier,
        "total_bytes": num_keys * (sizes.public_key + sizes.private_key) * multiplier,
        "total_mb": num_keys * (sizes.public_key + sizes.private_key) * multiplier / (1024 * 1024)
    }

def compare_migration(num_keys: int, 
                      classical: str, 
                      pqc: str) -> dict:
    """
    Compare storage before and after PQC migration.
    """
    classical_storage = calculate_storage(num_keys, classical)
    pqc_storage = calculate_storage(num_keys, pqc)
    
    return {
        "classical": classical_storage,
        "pqc": pqc_storage,
        "increase_factor": pqc_storage["total_bytes"] / classical_storage["total_bytes"],
        "additional_mb": pqc_storage["total_mb"] - classical_storage["total_mb"]
    }

# Example: Calculate storage for 10,000 keys
print("Storage Calculation for 10,000 keys")
print("=" * 50)

for alg in ["ECDSA-P256", "ML-DSA-65", "FN-DSA-512"]:
    result = calculate_storage(10000, alg)
    print(f"\n{alg}:")
    print(f"  Total: {result['total_mb']:.2f} MB")

# Migration comparison
print("\n" + "=" * 50)
print("Migration from ECDSA to ML-DSA-65 (10,000 keys)")
print("=" * 50)
comp = compare_migration(10000, "ECDSA-P256", "ML-DSA-65")
print(f"Storage increase: {comp['increase_factor']:.1f}x")
print(f"Additional storage needed: {comp['additional_mb']:.2f} MB")
exercises.expectedOutput: Working storage calculator with migration analysis
hints: Consider encryption overhead for stored keys

exercises.title: Key Lifecycle Manager
exercises.description: Implement a complete key lifecycle management system.
tasks: Define key states (pending, active, deprecated, destroyed)
Add time-based and usage-based rotation triggers
hints: Use state machine pattern for transitions
Log all state changes for audit
Check rotation conditions on each operation
Handle edge cases (key already destroyed, etc.)

exercises.title: Crypto Agility Abstraction Layer
exercises.description: Design and implement a crypto-agile abstraction layer for PQC algorithms.
tasks: Define algorithm-agnostic interfaces for signing and KEM
Create algorithm registry for runtime selection
Implement key format with algorithm identifiers
exercises.expectedOutput: Working crypto-agile abstraction with multiple algorithms
hints: Use factory pattern for algorithm instantiation
Store algorithm ID with keys for self-description
Version key formats for backward compatibility

exercises.title: Key Backup and Recovery Plan
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive key backup and recovery plan for PQC infrastructure.
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete key backup and recovery document
hints: Plan for both single key and bulk recovery
